body: Zeilen 89 bis 103 können in einem getCheckResult-Call gemacht werden.
Wäre das hier nicht einfacher zu lösen mit einem Array von KycDocument?
Hier ist zweimal derselbe KycState im Array. Und fehlt da nicht noch ein ! davor?
Ich denke nicht, dass das so funktioniert. Das getCustomerInformation gibt doch ein ganzes Objekt zurück?
The two `ValidateIf()` do IMO not change anything. What was the goal?
Need the same logic for cryptoRoute as for buy (only use targetDeposit address if there is one).
I would remove this check, this is actually not possible and would be handled by the DB as a fallback.
Talked to Robin, he wants the buyCrypto to be created directly after registration of the input (after line 63).
Update of buyId is only allowed, if bankTx is not null, cryptoRouteId otherwise.

Amount is in BTC but feeRate is in sat/vByte => need to multiply 0.000005 by 10^8. The line should like this:
```ts
return Math.min(fastestFee, 500 * amount);
```
Or am I wrong? 😉

amount is in BTC but feeRate is in sat/vByte. Need to divide the fee amount by 10^8. I would extract the fee amount calculation to a variable and add a comment (135 vByte for a single-input single-output TX),

```ts
console.error(`Failed to process Bitcoin input. No asset ${asset} found. UTXO:`, utxo);
```

body:
Good question ... I had to dig a little deeper 😉  I wanted the VM not to have access to other resources (DB, DeFiChain Nodes), therefore the two subnets.
But we could probably remove one NSG, as they are the same for both VMs.
It's still relevant. Instead of the asset list below, we could fetch the asset from database to get the category.
That's what I also thought ... then encountered this 😆
Ok agreed. `primary` or `reference` are not required, but `providers` should be 👍
I think this doesn't belong here but in lightning.service.ts (as it was before). There is no communication with our LND or LNbits.
`FORWARD` is probably not the right name. The logic is not about forwarding something 😉
Could be done in a for-loop 😉

I know you copied this code 😉 but why don't we actually use SQL to filter by dexName and blockchain? Like this

```ts
const pendingOrders = await this.liquidityOrderRepo.findBy({ isComplete: false, targetAsset: { dexName: 'BTC', blockchain: Blockchain.LIGHTNING } });
```
I think it could make sense to move this to the PayoutLightningService, it would reduce the code.
Use Date objects and date methods from Util.ts
I think this "method-forwarding" is not necessary.
Use approxPriceChf, check usages on develop?

I think the where should look like this:
```ts
      .where('user.id = :id', { id })
      .andWhere('wallet.isKycClient = 0')
      .andWhere('linkedUser.status != :blocked', { blocked: UserStatus.BLOCKED })
```
Good point. For now it should only be used for transactions with real IBANs. We will need to find a solution for all transactions later.
Apparently, yes. You only need it in the relations, if you want the data to be returned. For filtering it's not required.
Do we need to use the inputCurrency to get the min volume (it might depend on the currency)?
Hehe, that's not my code 😆  but you are right 👍
The unassigned transaction will only appear, if the IBAN matches, so this case should never happen. And the endpoint is not publicly visible, only for DFX Services.
It's actually adapted to the automated validation error messages
Maybe use `DFX` as source instead of `Exchange`?
I left it out on purpose because we now basically understand the process of the webhooks and don't need the other webhooks at all.

body: This fetch is unnecessary, we only use the id of the user
Check if order is not null
The rule will change it's status to `Processing` when a pipeline is running
We normally have the date at the beginning of the name, and in short format 🤔  e.g. like this: `20250402-onboarding-0-311135-211146.pdf`

Currently the file names look like this: `20250402-NameCheck-0-311124-163302.pdf`, I think we should keep the format more or less?

20250402 is the date
311124 is the user data ID
163302 is the time

+
+  // --- Pending Review Queries ---
+
+  async getPendingReviewSummary(): Promise<{ name: KycStepName; status: ReviewStatus; count: number }[]> {
+    return this.kycStepRepo
+      .createQueryBuilder('step')
+      .select('step.name', 'name')
+      .addSelect('step.status', 'status')
+      .addSelect('COUNT(step.id)', 'count')
+      .innerJoin('step.userData', 'userData')
+      .where('step.status IN (:...statuses)', { statuses: [ReviewStatus.MANUAL_REVIEW, ReviewStatus.INTERNAL_REVIEW] })
+      .andWhere('userData.status != :mergedStatus', { mergedStatus: UserDataStatus.MERGED })
+      .groupBy('step.name')
+      .addGroupBy('step.status')
+      .getRawMany<{ name: KycStepName; status: ReviewStatus; count: string }>()
+      .then((rows) => rows.map((r) => ({ name: r.name, status: r.status, count: Number(r.count) })));
+  }
+
+  async getPendingReviewSteps(name: KycStepName, status: ReviewStatus): Promise<KycStep[]> {
+    return this.kycStepRepo
+      .createQueryBuilder('step')
+      .innerJoinAndSelect('step.userData', 'userData')
+      .where('step.name = :name', { name })
+      .andWhere('step.status = :status', { status })
+      .andWhere('userData.status != :mergedStatus', { mergedStatus: UserDataStatus.MERGED })
+      .orderBy('step.updated', 'ASC')
+      .getMany();

body: Can be done w/o query builder?
Is it the hex or the envelope?

Can't we use the [@dfx.swiss/bip322-multisig](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dfx.swiss/bip322-multisig) package here?

I would return undefined instead of an empty array. This way, the front end knows whether the data should not be displayed or whether there are simply no entries yet -> no need to mirror the settings in the UI.
